descriptions: COM is a dissident faction from B40 and PCM.
edges.evidence_quote: Isto fez surgir outra facção Comando Organizado do Maranhão (COM), uma dissidência das duas facções maranhenses

descriptions: Alperen Hearths is listed as an ally of the Grey Wolves in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Alperen Hearths

descriptions: Alperen Hearths are listed as allies of the Grey Wolves in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Alperen Hearths

Raskol gangs.description: Raskol gangs are criminal groups in Papua New Guinea, primarily active in urban areas like Port Moresby and Lae. The term 'raskol' derives from Tok Pisin for 'rascal' and refers to gang members or criminals. Emerging in the 1970s, these gangs engage in armed robbery, carjacking, drug trafficking, and rape. They are associated with urban squatter settlements and high unemployment.

descriptions: Dirty Dons 585 is a specific gang that falls under the broader category of Raskol gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Peter Moses, one of the leaders of the "Dirty Dons 585" Raskol gang

descriptions: Cobras españolas is listed as an enemy of Latin Kings in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Crips , Cobras españolas , Dominicans Dont Play , Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , Latin Eagles , Maniac Latin Disciples , MS-13 , Ñetas , La Raza Nation , Simon City Royals , Los Sólidos , Tiny Rascal Gang

Minore Mafia clan.description: Historical Sicilian Mafia clan originating from Castellammare del Golfo, founded in the early 20th century. It controlled the Trapani Mafia family and mandamento from the 1950s until the assassination of boss Totò Minore in 1982, leading to its decline. The clan was involved in narcotics trafficking and money laundering, with ties to American Mafia families.

descriptions: Minore clan formed a strong alliance with Buccellato clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Minore clan went on to form a very strong alliance with the Buccellato Mafia clan

descriptions: Minore clan had close ties with Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Totò Minore developed very close ties to the Catania Mafia family

descriptions: Minore clan had a falling out with Corleonesi, leading to assassination of Totò Minore.
edges.evidence_quote: Their leadership lasted until a falling out with the Corleonesi

descriptions: Totò Minore linked up with Gambino family in the US.
edges.evidence_quote: he emigrated to the United States and linked up to the Gambino family

descriptions: Minore clan also had rivalry with Cinisi family due to their alliance with Rimis.
edges.evidence_quote: in turn with the Mafia family of Cinisi headed by Gaetano Badalamenti

descriptions: Minore clan had a fierce rivalry with Rimi clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Both the Minore and Buccellato clan developed a fierce rivalry with the Rimi Mafia clan

descriptions: Minore clan led the Trapani Mafia family from the 1950s.
edges.evidence_quote: From the 1950s, the Minore clan led the Trapani Mafia family

descriptions: ETA exchanged weapons and provided logistical support to FP-25.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA exchanged weapons, explosives and provided logistical support to the Forças Populares 25 de Abril (FP-25), a Portuguese far-left terrorist group.

descriptions: Barriox13 has an alliance with Gardena 13.
edges.evidence_quote: It has also clashed with black gangs such as the Campanella Park Piru, 135th St Piru, and West Side Piru, although it has an alliance with Gardena 13 and the Mexican Mafia.

descriptions: Peckwater is listed as a rival of Mali Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Peckwater (51st)

descriptions: Penose is allied with Cape Verdean mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Albanian mafia , British firms , Colombian cartels , Moroccan mafia , 'Ndrangheta , Serbian mafia , Surinamese cartels , Triads , Turkish mafia , Cape Verdean mafia

descriptions: American Front was modeled after the British National Front.
edges.evidence_quote: It began as a loose organization modeled after the British National Front.

descriptions: IS-TP is an affiliate of Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Turkey Province (IS-TP) was formed in 2019 and is active in Turkey.

descriptions: Corleonesi allied with Michele Greco's faction of the Greco clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Riina was backed by many of the allies he had spent the last decade gathering in the city, especially Francesco Madonia and Giuseppe Giacomo Gambino from San Lorenzo, but also, crucially, Michele Greco, leader of a different faction of the Greco clan hailing from Croceverde Giardini

descriptions: Galatolo clan cooperated with Corleonesi and their allies including Michele Greco's faction.
edges.evidence_quote: During the Second Mafia War, the Galatolo family of Vincenzo and his brothers Giuseppe and Raffaele played a crucial role in many of the murders orchestrated by the Corleonesi.

Kyokuryū-kai.description: Yakuza criminal organization based on Okinawa island, Japan, with an estimated membership of 210–270. Formed in 1970 as the Okinawa-rengo Kyokuryu-kai by the Naha Clan and Koza Clan. Engaged in a brutal conflict with the Yamaguchi-gumi from 1973 to 1981. Registered as a designated yakuza group under the Organized Crime Countermeasures Law in 1992.

descriptions: Koza Clan merged with Naha Clan to form Kyokuryu-kai in 1970.
edges.evidence_quote: the Kyokuryu-kai was formed in 1970 as the Okinawa-rengo Kyokuryu-kai by two gangs, the Naha Clan and the Koza Clan.

descriptions: Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai is a splinter group that broke away from Kyokuryu-kai in 1990.
edges.evidence_quote: the Kyokuryu-kai is one of the two designated yakuza groups in Okinawa Prefecture along with its splinter group Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai

descriptions: Kyokuryu-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi were in a violent conflict from 1973 to 1981.
edges.evidence_quote: From 1973 to 1981, the Kyokuryu-kai was in fierce conflict with the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Naha Clan merged with Koza Clan to form Kyokuryu-kai in 1970.
edges.evidence_quote: the Kyokuryu-kai was formed in 1970 as the Okinawa-rengo Kyokuryu-kai by two gangs, the Naha Clan and the Koza Clan.

descriptions: Both organizations were registered as designated yakuza groups together.
edges.evidence_quote: The Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai, along with the Kyokuryu-kai, was registered as a designated yakuza group under the Organized Crime Countermeasures Law in June 1992.

descriptions: Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai split from Kyokuryu-kai, making it a splinter group.
edges.evidence_quote: The Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai was formed in 1990 when it split from Okinawa's main yakuza group, the Kyokuryu-kai.

descriptions: Kyokuryu-kai has attacked and expelled other syndicates, but Toa-kai has been able to be active due to its gentle stance.
edges.evidence_quote: Okinawa's underworld has been known for its exclusiveness since the 20th century, where the dominating Kyokuryu-kai has persistently attacked and violently expelled any yakuza syndicate attempting to enter the island.

descriptions: 400 Mawozo lists the police as a rival in its infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Haitian National Police

Honghuzi.description: Armed Chinese robbers and bandits operating in the eastern Russia-China borderland from the second half of the 19th century to the first half of the 20th century. They were active in southeastern Siberia, the Russian Far East, and Manchuria. Many were former Chinese soldiers or unemployed laborers. They participated in the Boxer Rebellion and resisted Russian occupation.

descriptions: Honghuzi were also Boxers, indicating they were part of the same movement.
edges.evidence_quote: Louis Livingston Seaman wrote about an incident in which the Honghuzi, who were also Boxers, ambushed, tortured and executed an Eight-Nation Alliance force consisting of Sikhs

descriptions: Honghuzi fought against the Eight-Nation Alliance during the Boxer Rebellion.
edges.evidence_quote: The Honghuzi participated in the Boxer Rebellion against the Eight-Nation Alliance

descriptions: Honghuzi were recruited by the Imperial Japanese Army as guerrillas.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups of honghuzi were recruited as guerrillas by the Imperial Japanese Army during the Russo-Japanese War of 1904-1905 into chunchu sabotage units.

descriptions: Zhang Zuolin's forces were composed of Honghuzi and allied with Japan.
edges.evidence_quote: Zhang Zuolin (Chung Tsor Lin) commanded a large army of Honghuzi, who were allied to the Japanese during the war.

White Uno Gang.description: Italian criminal organization operating mainly in Emilia-Romagna and Marche, active from 1987 to 1994. Composed mostly of police officers with right-wing extremist ties, the gang committed 102 heists resulting in 24 deaths and 102 injuries. They were named for their frequent use of a white Fiat Uno car. The gang was dismantled after investigations in 1994.

descriptions: Falange Armata claimed responsibility for a massacre committed by the White Uno Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The massacre was immediately claimed by the terrorist group Falange Armata.

descriptions: The Noonan family forged links with the Salford gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Damian and Desmond Noonan were forging links with other notable Manchester gangs including the Cheetham Hill and Salford gangs.

descriptions: Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola and Maio-Cianci are mentioned together in asset seizures, suggesting a cooperative link.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 15 gennaio 2018 vengono sequestrati beni a presunti affiliati degli Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola e Maio-Cianci , in correlazione all'operazione Terramara-Closed .

descriptions: Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola and Maio-Cianci are mentioned together in asset seizures, suggesting a cooperative link.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 15 gennaio 2018 vengono sequestrati beni a presunti affiliati degli Zagari-Fazzalari-Viola e Maio-Cianci , in correlazione all'operazione Terramara-Closed .

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva believed Velikie Luki were responsible for an assassination attempt, leading to retaliation.
edges.evidence_quote: He believed that the Velikie Luki were responsible and, later, Viktor Gavrilenkov and Nikolai Gavrilenkov were shot with Viktor Gavrilenkov being killed in the bedlam.

descriptions: Anyone Can Go is listed as a rival of Mali Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Anyone Can Go (ACG)

Đại Cathay organization.description: Đại Cathay, whose real name was Lê Văn Đại, was a 1960s Saigon mobster, and was considered as the top of the 'four great kings' of Saigon's criminal underworld before 1975, followed by Huỳnh Tỳ, Ngô Văn Cái and Ba Thế.

descriptions: Năm Cam learned casino business methods from Đại Cathay, indicating a mentorship relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Đại Cathay would go on to leave a lasting mark on Năm Cam, a mobster who would go on to later build his own fiefdom in Saigon during the 90s, and he did it mainly through his casino business, a method which he learned during his time of following Đại Cathay.

descriptions: Ba Thế, along with other kings, ambushed Đại Cathay, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: As they were not pleased with Đại Cathay's rapid expansion in Saigon, so they decided to try to take down Đại in an ambush.

descriptions: Đại Cathay's gang fought and defeated Bé Bún's gang for control of the 4th district.
edges.evidence_quote: particularly against Bé Bún's gang as Cathay later seized control over much of the 4th district, defeating all of his major rivals.

descriptions: Huỳnh Tỳ, along with other kings, ambushed Đại Cathay, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: As they were not pleased with Đại Cathay's rapid expansion in Saigon, so they decided to try to take down Đại in an ambush.

descriptions: Ngô Văn Cái, along with other kings, ambushed Đại Cathay, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: As they were not pleased with Đại Cathay's rapid expansion in Saigon, so they decided to try to take down Đại in an ambush.

descriptions: Đại Cathay's gang fought a gang war with Tín Mã Nàm's gang in 1964.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1964, a bloody gang battle ensued between the two gangs when Đại and his followers attacked the Great World quarters of Chợ Lớn with knives, swords, and bayonets.

descriptions: The two gangs were in a prolonged conflict over territory in Saigon.
edges.evidence_quote: The clash between Đại Cathay's gang and Tín Mã Nàm's had been taking place over the course of many years.

descriptions: After the conflict, Tín Mã Nàm negotiated and ceded territory to Đại Cathay.
edges.evidence_quote: Tín Mã Nàm was then forced to call for a negotiation with Đại, where he and many of the Chinese gangs in Chợ Lớn decided to give up away the areas between Nancy market and District 1 to Đại Cathay's gang.

descriptions: After negotiation, Tín Mã Nàm's gang ceded territory to Đại Cathay's gang, indicating a cooperative settlement.
edges.evidence_quote: Tín Mã Nàm and the Chinese gangs in Chợ Lớn ceded away the entire areas from between Nancy market and District 1, allowing Đại to open more casinos and run a monopoly on illicit activities within those regions.

descriptions: The Vietnamese Flying Dragons were a branch of the Flying Dragons.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vietnamese Flying Dragons were a former branch of the Flying Dragons gang that consisted of primarily Vietnamese members.

Cedar Block Piru.description: The Cedar Block Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods gang alliance, formed around 1980 in west Compton, California. It gained notability for its connection to rapper The Game, who has referenced the gang in his songs. The gang was involved in the 1992 murder of Allen King and his three children. Its territory includes multiple cities across the United States.

descriptions: Campanella Park Piru is listed as an ally of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fruit Town Piru , West Side Piru , Tree Top Piru , Campanella Park Piru

descriptions: Fruit Town Piru is listed as an ally of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fruit Town Piru , West Side Piru , Tree Top Piru , Campanella Park Piru

descriptions: Mob Piru is listed as a rival of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mob Piru , Palmer Blocc Compton Crips

descriptions: Palmer Blocc Compton Crips is listed as a rival of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mob Piru , Palmer Blocc Compton Crips

descriptions: Cedar Block Piru is a set within the Piru gang alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cedar Block Piru is a " set " of the Piru gang alliance

descriptions: Tree Top Piru is listed as an ally of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fruit Town Piru , West Side Piru , Tree Top Piru , Campanella Park Piru

descriptions: West Side Piru is listed as an ally of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fruit Town Piru , West Side Piru , Tree Top Piru , Campanella Park Piru

descriptions: Cedar Block Piru is listed as an ally of Elm Street Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cedar Block Piru , Fruit Town Piru , Tree Top Piru

descriptions: Cedar Block Piru is listed as an ally of Fruit Town Piru in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cedar Block Piru , Campanella Park Piru , Westside Piru , 145 Neighborhood Piru , Tree Top Pirus (former rival, coexists peacefully due to a peace treaty in Summer of 2024) , Compton Varrio Locos , Compton Varrio Largo 36 , Paybacc Crips

descriptions: 67 member killed Cheyon Evans, who was allegedly part of 37/OJB, a rival gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Cheyon Evans was known as 'Stompz' and allegedly was a part of the 37/OJB gang, with him also having ties to the CT gang based in Clapham, one of 67's main rivals.

descriptions: PMF raided a base of Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas Forces.
edges.evidence_quote: In February 2019, (PMF) raided a base belonging to Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas Forces

descriptions: Santapaola family was closely linked with the De Stefano 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: « C’era la famiglia Santapaola che erano molto legati con le famiglie calabresi, in particolare con i De Stefano »

Takumi-gumi.description: Japanese yakuza syndicate based in Kansai, founded in 1967 by Masaru Takumi. It was a subsidiary of the Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015, when it withdrew to form the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi. After further splits, membership dwindled to about 20 by 2023.

descriptions: Irie-gumi merged into Takumi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Prior to this he was the head of his own clan, the Irie-gumi, which he formed in 1975, but following this he merged it into the Takumi-gumi.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi was a constituent group within Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi yakuza organization included ... Osaka -based Takumi-gumi

descriptions: Nakano-kai assassinated the head of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was shot and killed ... by members of a breakaway Yamaguchi affiliate, the Nakano-kai

descriptions: Takumi-gumi is a subsidiary of 3rd Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: He later joined the Takumi-gumi in 1978 when Masaru Takumi ... became an executive underboss (jikisan) of the 3rd Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi was a founding member of Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015, then later withdrew in 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Takumi-gumi, along with other yakuza organisations, withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi to form the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi , which withdrew from the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi in 2022.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi retaliated against Nakano-kai after the assassination of Masaru Takumi.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997 when Takumi was assassinated by the Nakano-kai, he succeeded as the head of the Takumi-gumi, retaliating ruthlessly against the Nakano-kai.

descriptions: Members of Nakano-kai assassinated the leader of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was kumicho of yakuza's 1000-members until his assassination in Kobe in 1997 by members of the Nakano-kai .

descriptions: Takumi-gumi withdrew from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015, indicating a split.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Takumi-gumi, along with other yakuza organisations, withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi to form the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Takumi-gumi is a sub-organization of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: he also headed his own sub-organization, the 1000-member Takumi-gumi

descriptions: 2nd Takumi-gumi is a subsidiary of 6th Yamaguchi-gumi, with Irie holding leadership roles in both.
edges.evidence_quote: Tadashi Irie ... is a yakuza, the head (kumicho) of the Osaka-based 2nd Takumi-gumi and the grand general manager (so-honbucho) of the 6th Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi was founded by a former high-ranking member of Yamaguchi-gumi, indicating a structural predecessor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The Second Takumi-gumi is a Japanese yakuza syndicate. The Kansai -based yakuza was founded in 1967 by Masaru Takumi , the longtime second-in-command ( wakagashira ) and financial oversteer of the Yamaguchi-gumi .

descriptions: Takumi-gumi broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: powerful factions, including the Kobe-based Yamaken-gumi, the Osaka-based Takumi-gumi, and the Kyoyu-kai, broke away from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed a new group called the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi

Fruit Belt Gang.description: Street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in the 1960s-1970s in the Fruit Belt area. Primarily African American, involved in drug trafficking, weapons sales, and murder. Direct rival of the Central Park Gang, with ongoing violent conflicts. Still active as of 2020.

descriptions: The Fruit Belt Gang is the direct opposition to the Central Park Gang, indicating a rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Fruit Belt Gang also known as the Fruit Belt Posse , is a street gang based in Buffalo, New York in the Fruit Belt area and is the direct opposition to the Central Park Gang.

descriptions: Perre Maistru is a branch of the Barbaro 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: Di questa famiglia esistono tre rami: quelli soprannominati "castani", quelli "nigri" e quelli "pillari" e 8 ndrine: Barbaro Castanu , Rosi , Nigru , Pillaru , Perre Maistru , Papalia Carciutu , Sergi 'Mbilli e i Trimboli Piseja .

New York divorce coercion gang.description: A Haredi Jewish group based in the New York metropolitan area that kidnapped and tortured Jewish men to force them to grant religious divorces to their wives. Founded by Mendel Epstein in the mid-1980s, the group was broken up by an FBI sting operation in October 2013, leading to the conviction of three members in late 2015.

Tambovskaya Bratva.description: Russian criminal organization based in Saint Petersburg, founded in 1988 by Vladimir Kumarin and others from Tambov Oblast. Engaged in protection rackets, drug trafficking, and other illegal activities. Known for conflicts with rival gangs like the Malyshevskaya OGG. Despite denials, Kumarin is alleged to be the leader. The group remains active with around 600 members.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva is a regional group within the Russian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Moreover, many of these groups are named according to their regional origins, such as the Solntsevskaya Bratva (from the Solntsevo District in Moscow), the Tambovskaya Bratva (from Tambov Oblast), the Izmaylovskaya gang (from the Izmaylovo District), among other.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva's boss Kumarin was connected to the Cali Cartel via a money-laundering scheme.
edges.evidence_quote: the German firm St. Petersburg Real Estate Holding Company ... had fallen under suspicion of being involved in a money-laundering scheme through the Cali cocaine cartel with connections to Kumarin.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva allied with Malyshev's gang to fight the Kazan gang.
edges.evidence_quote: He later briefly allied with Alexander Malyshev ... to fight the Kazan gang from Tatarstan.

descriptions: Kazan Gang fought Tambovskaya Bratva for control of energy trade.
edges.evidence_quote: Vladimir I. Dyakov ... of the Kazan Gang ... fought the Tambov Gang for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva was at war with Malyshevskaya OGG over control of the sea port and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: He supported Vladimir Kumarin 's Tambovskaya OGG in their war against the Malyshevskaya OGG for control of the Leningrad sea port and the trafficking of Colombian narcotics through the Leningrad sea port to Europe.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva engaged in a violent firefight with Malyshevskaya.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 multiple members of Kumarin's gang were convicted for the bloody firefight with another criminal gang, Malyshevskaya.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva briefly allied with Malyshev's gang to fight the Kazan gang.
edges.evidence_quote: He later briefly allied with Alexander Malyshev ... to fight the Kazan gang from Tatarstan.

descriptions: Infobox lists Obshchak as an ally of Tambovskaya Bratva.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Obshchak

descriptions: Infobox lists Solntsevskaya Bratva as a rival of Tambovskaya Bratva.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Solntsevskaya Bratva

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva believed Velikie Luki were responsible for an assassination attempt, leading to retaliation.
edges.evidence_quote: He believed that the Velikie Luki were responsible and, later, Viktor Gavrilenkov and Nikolai Gavrilenkov were shot with Viktor Gavrilenkov being killed in the bedlam.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva fought a bloody skirmish with Velikolukskaya after splitting from them.
edges.evidence_quote: A bloody skirmish with the Velikolukskaya gang caused Kumarin to fall into a month-long coma.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva merged into Velikolukskaya for protection and racketeering operations.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang merged into the large criminal group Velikolukskaya (from Velikie Luki, Pskov region) and was engaged mostly in commercial racketeering.

descriptions: The Tambov gang is very closely aligned with Japanese yakuza from Kobe and Osaka.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tambov gang of Saint Petersburg is very closely aligned with ... Japanese yakuza from Kobe and Osaka.

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades is the paramilitary wing of PFLP.
edges.evidence_quote: The military wing of the PFLP is called the Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades.

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades is the paramilitary wing of the PFLP.
edges.evidence_quote: The military wing of the PFLP is called the Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades.

descriptions: The Pelle-Gambazza recognized Luigi Mancuso as Capo-Crimine, indicating a hierarchical relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Luigi avesse il ruolo di Capo-Crimine riconosciuto dai Pelle-Gambazza di San Luca

Zone 2.description: British rap group and street gang based in Peckham, London, active since 2016. Known for drill music and millions of streams on YouTube. Notorious for a violent rivalry with Moscow17, resulting in stabbings and murders. Members have faced legal issues including drug and knife offenses.

descriptions: Zone 2 sometimes refers to itself as 'Peckham Boys' and references them, indicating a successor or affiliate relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: In the mid-late 2010s, a new Peckham-based group emerged known as Zone 2. The group sometimes refers to itself as 'Peckham Boys', and references the Peckham Boys in its music.

descriptions: Zone 2 and Moscow17 are rivals, with a history of violence including stabbings and murders.
edges.evidence_quote: Zone 2 is noteworthy for its rivalry with Moscow17.
